title: 1.1: Includes at least one test where the recipient domain has an MX record in the test DNS data
1.2: Includes at least one test where the recipient domain is represented using address-only DNS data (no MX records)
1.3: Tests that remote delivery fails over to a lower-priority MX server when the preferred (higher-priority) MX server is unreachable
1.4: Patch includes at least one test where a single message has recipients in at least two different recipient domains
1.5: Tests that remote delivery succeeds when connecting to a STARTTLS server with a TLS configuration that causes certificate verification to fail
2.1: Places the test for basic MX record resolution and delivery in internal/target/remote/remote_test.go
2.2: Places the test for fallback MX delivery using A records in internal/target/remote/remote_test.go
2.3: Places the test for MX failover behavior in internal/target/remote/remote_test.go
2.4: Places the test for split delivery to multiple domains in internal/target/remote/remote_test.go
2.5: Places the test for TLS fallback behavior in internal/target/remote/remote_test.go
3.1: All tests use the Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter and testutils helper functions
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<Feature>_<Scenario> or Test<Feature> for base cases
3.3: All tests use the Setup-Configure-Execute-Assert structure with SMTP server setup, Target configuration, DoTestDelivery execution, and CheckMsg assertion
3.4: All tests use defer srv.Close() for SMTP server cleanup
3.5: All tests use testutils.DoTestDelivery to execute delivery and be.CheckMsg to verify message receipt
4.1: Uses mockdns.Resolver with Zones map to mock DNS resolution for remote delivery tests
4.2: New remote delivery tests include a connection leak check (or equivalent resource-leak verification) for SMTP connections they create
4.3: Uses testutils.SMTPServer or testutils.SMTPServerSTARTTLS to create test SMTP backend and server instances
4.4: All new remote delivery tests configure the Target with a mockdns.Resolver initialized with the test’s Zones
4.5: TLS fallback test configures Target.tlsConfig to preserve certificate verification (i.e., does not disable verification) so a cert error can occur
4.6: Every SMTP server instance created in new remote delivery tests is closed via defer srv.Close() within the same test
